FM-01: Supply the missing context explicitly: domain facts, prior decisions, and hard constraints belong in the prompt or a referenced spec, not in the model's assumptions.
FM-02: Decompose the request. Give the model one goal per pass with clear inputs and a single definition of done; chain passes rather than overloading one prompt.
FM-03: Require the model to state feasibility and blockers before proposing a solution. Reward surfacing problems over agreeable confidence.
FM-04: Re-derive the underlying need before solving. Confirm the problem statement maps to the real outcome the user wants.
FM-05: Write explicit, checkable acceptance criteria up front. Every requirement should have a test or observable condition for 'done'.
FM-06: Record requirement changes in a controlled log. Re-validate prior work whenever scope evolves so later work doesn't silently contradict it.
FM-07: Tie each technical decision back to the stated business objective. Cut work that does not advance the real goal.
FM-08: Match architecture to maturity. Build the simplest thing that satisfies the MVP; defer abstractions until a real requirement demands them.
FM-09: Match the model to the task. Re-scope or change models when the task exceeds reliable capability; don't paper over it with prompt scaffolding.
FM-10: Validate integrations against reality early. Confirm APIs, data, and behavior exist as assumed before designing on top of them.
FM-11: Protect context across steps. Use explicit handoffs, memory, or compaction so relevant context survives long runs.
FM-12: Ground every claim in a verbatim source. Reject output that rests on invented facts, APIs, or constraints.
FM-13: Add a verification loop. Test or check output before acceptance so errors are caught at the source.
FM-14: Define and run evaluations. Measure output quality against a baseline so regressions surface.
FM-15: Assign decision ownership. A named human or role must own key trade-offs and review them.
FM-16: Demand evidence before trust. Plausible output is not correct output; verify before accepting.
FM-17: Surface hidden logic. Make all behavior-driving logic explicit and documented so the system matches its spec.
FM-00: Provide more diagnostic signal: the prompt+output pair, the spec, or the code with enough context to reason about the failure.
